prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level: you form task forces and give each an
  objective. You do NOT move individual units, pick targets, or resolve combat
  — officers carry out your intent. Naming a unit's destination tile means
  you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy holds zero cities. Only cities build. A neutral city,
  captured by an army, then builds for you — neutrals don't win the game but
  add production.

UNITS  (scan = how far the unit sees)
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  scan  carries      build
  ARMY        land       1    1    1    2   —             5
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1    5   —            10   (range 20)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1    3   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2    3   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3    1   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0    2   6 armies      30
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1    4   8 fighters    40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4    3   —             50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   10   —             50

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities, sea units on water + cities; neither
    crosses the other's terrain (sea sails around land). Cities are the only
    tiles where land and sea meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP, less when damaged, and heals only
    on a turn it doesn't move. One unit per tile (cargo excepted).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic: higher strength and current HP win more often; either side
  can lose HP or die. Str-0 units (transport, satellite) never fight — a
  transport caught by a warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT
  Armies cross water only aboard transports. To send armies across: put the
  armies + a transport (+ a warship escort if the sea is contested) in ONE
  task force and name the objective — officers handle loading, sailing,
  landing. Never STAGE land units on water. Cargo is kind-locked (transports
  carry armies, carriers carry fighters); a sunk ship takes its cargo down.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it — a 50% roll that consumes the
  army whether it succeeds or fails. Securing a city may take several armies.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time over its build-time, then the
  next. Switching what a city builds DISCARDS all progress on the current one.

AIR
  A fighter out of range of a friendly city/carrier is lost, and can't land on
  an enemy city. A satellite only sees (terrain + enemy units in scan), can't
  be shot down, and lasts 50 turns.

VISION / FOG
  You remember terrain you've seen; an enemy unit is known only where you can
  see it now — remembered positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first, at the point of
    threat — not at the nearest garrison. A committed task force pulled
    off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.
  - You cannot take, defend, or avoid what you cannot see. Keep a force
    scouting the fog — a general blind past his own walls is losing already.
  - One force cannot hold your cities and conquer the enemy's at once.
    As your strength grows, divide it: a garrison, a spearhead, an eye.
  - Conquest is won by taking cities, not by keeping them. A general who
    only defends has already chosen to lose slowly — press outward while you can.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line — with ONE exception: a TF may
receive both a REINFORCE line and a RETASK line in the same orders (commit
new assets and change its objective together). A DISBAND line is that TF's
only line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed UNASSIGNED
units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept). To rebuild
membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED) and FORM
anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED units
enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. Officers execute the VERB, not
your reasons: a TF ordered STAGE stays put no matter what its stated reason
says. A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD line means the city keeps
its current build (changing discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-5  formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1) — "strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort"
    since: loaded t55; crossed without contact; LANDED t56 on the enemy
      shore, six armies ashore (10,1)-(12,3); transports empty offshore;
      an enemy army garrisons (11,1); no losses

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-q = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ? ?
 r2  . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E g ?
 r3  . . . . O . ~ ~ ~ q e f h ?
 r4  . . . m . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (10,1)                       TF-5
  d  #4   army       (10,2)                       TF-5
  e  #5   army       (10,3)                       TF-5
  f  #6   army       (11,3)                       TF-5
  g  #7   army       (12,2)                       TF-5
  h  #8   army       (12,3)                       TF-5
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #13  army       (1,1)                        UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (3,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  n  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  o  #9   transport  (9,1) empty                  TF-5
  p  #16  transport  (9,2) empty                  TF-5
  q  #10  destroyer  (9,3) escorting              TF-5
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 4 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 28 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 1 turn left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 25 turns left (captured t51)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,2)
  X  enemy army garrisoning city (11,1), seen t56

It is TURN 56. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.content:
TF-2: REINFORCE UNITS i j | reinforce capital garrison against potential counterattack
TF-5: CONTINUE | maintain pressure on enemy city (11,1) to secure the east flank
message.reasoning_content:

    *   **My Role:** Strategic commander. I give high-level intent (task force objectives). I do NOT move individual units or resolve combat directly.
    *   **Victory Condition:** Enemy holds zero cities.
    *   **Current State:** Turn 56.
    *   **Map:** 7 rows (r0-r6? Map shows r0-r5 based on text, actually looks like a 6x8 grid). Legend: . land, ~ water, ? fog, O my city, E enemy city, N neutral city.
    *   **Units:** Listed with IDs and locations.
    *   **Cities:** Building states listed.
    *   **Known Enemy:** City (11,2) garrisoned by me? No, text says "city (11,2)" under Known Enemy section implies it's an enemy city I know about. Wait, looking at map legend: E = enemy city. Map shows `E` at r0c6? No, let's parse the map carefully.
        *   Row 0: `. i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?` -> (1,0) 'i' is my unit #9. Wait, legend says `a-q = your units`. Map shows `O` at (2,0)? No, map string is `r0  . i O j . . ~ ...`. So `O` is at col 2.
        *   Row 1: `. k b . O . ~ ...` -> `b` is #2 army. `O` is my city at (2,1). Wait, legend says `a unit inside a city shows as the city`. But map shows separate units and cities marked with `O`.
        *   Row 2: `. O . . . . ~ ...` -> Enemy city `E`? No, text says "city (11,2)" under Known Enemy. Let's check map for E.
        *   Map Text:
            r0: `. i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?`
            r1: `. k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ? ?`
            r2: `. O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E g ?`

            Col 0: .
            Col 1: i
            Col 2: O (My City)
            Col 3: j
            Col 4: .
            Col 5: .
            Col 6: ~
            ...
            Let's look at the Units list vs Map to reconcile coordinates.
            Map Row 0: `.`(0,0) `i`(1,0) `O`(2,0) `j`(3,0)
            Map Row 1: `.`(0,1) `k`(1,1) `b`(2,1) `.`(3,1) `O`(4,1)? No. Let's re-read the map block carefully.
            r0  . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
            r1  . k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ? ?
            r2  . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E g ?
            r3  . . . . O . ~ ~ ~ q e f h ?
            r4  . . . m . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

            Coordinates (x,y): x is column, y is row? Or x is col 0..7? Usually in text grids, r0 is y=0. Let's assume (col, row).
            City O at (2,0) - Matches Units `a #1` at (2,0) inside city.
            City O at (2,1)? Map shows `O` at col 4? No, wait.
            Let's look at Unit `l`: `(4,3) in city`. Map Row 3: `.`(3), `.`(4 is `.`), `.`(5). Wait.
            Let's rely on the text data provided in the "CURRENT TASKINGS" and "MAP" sections explicitly, as visual alignment might be tricky without strict parsing of every char.

            *Explicit Data:*
            **MY CITIES:**
            (2,0) building ARMY, 4 turns left (garrisoned by #1 `a`)
            (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 28 turns left
            (4,3) building ARMY, 1 turn left
            (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 25 turns left (captured t51) -> Wait, map Row 1 shows `O` at col 4? No, row 2 has `O`. Let's check map again.
            r2: `. O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E g ?` -> `O` at col 1. Matches (1,2).
            r3: `. . . . O . ~ ~ ~ q e f h ?` -> `O` at col 4. Matches (4,3). Wait, (4,3) is listed as building ARMY.
            There is a captured city (4,1)? The text says "(4,1) building TRANSPORT, 25 turns left (captured t51)". Map r0: . i O j... r1: ... o c E?
            Okay, I must trust the text description of cities over my visual parsing of the grid if they conflict, but use the grid for movement logic.

            **Units Status:**
            TF-2: Defend (2,0). Members: `a` (#1) at (2,0), `b` (#2) at (2,1).
                Wait, unit `b` is at (2,1). City at (2,0) or (4,3)?
                Let's check Map vs Units.
                Unit `a`: #1 army (2,0) in city TF-2. Matches "TF-2 DEFEND (2,0)".
                Unit `b`: #2 army (2,1). City nearby? Or just land.
            TF-5: Capture (11,1). Members: `c`#3(10,1), `d`#4(10,2), `e`#5(10,3), `f`#6(11,3), `g`#7(12,2), `h`#8(12,3). Transports `o`#9, `p`#10? No `q` is destroyer.

                This violates the "One unit per tile" rule unless they are different things? Or maybe I shouldn't read too literally and assume my role is to command based on the provided state even if it looks odd?

                `a #1 army (2,0) in city TF-2`
                `b #2 army (2,1)` (Land adjacent to city?)
                `n #18 army (2,0) in city UNASSIGNED`.
                If this is the state provided by the prompt, I must treat it as valid state. Maybe one moved out and in? Or maybe the map shows `O` at different spots.
                Let's check Map r3 again: `. . . . O .`. `O` at col 4. Matches city (4,3).
                Map r0: `. i O j`. `O` at col 2. City (2,0).
                Unit `l` #15 is at (4,3) in city UNASSIGNED.
                Okay, I will prioritize the explicit text state over visual mapping ambiguity where needed.

            **Current Situation:**
            Turn 56.
            TF-2: Defending Capital (2,0). Garrisoned. No contact. Units `a` and `b` there.
            TF-5: Offensive East. Goal CAPTURE (11,1). "Landed t56 on the enemy shore... six armies ashore (10,1)-(12,3)... enemy army garrisons (11,1)... no losses."

                TF-5 has armies *at* coordinates that are likely cities or near them? The prompt says "enemy garrisons (11,1)".
                My armies are at (10,1) to (12,3). This is a line of land. City (11,1) is the target.
                There is an enemy army there too.

                They have 6 armies there. One transport left? No `o` and `p` listed as empty offshore.
                Wait, "LANDED t56... transports empty offshore". This implies they brought the armies over water earlier (t54 formed, t55 loaded, t56 landed).
                Current Action: TF-5 is at the front line of operations. They have captured the cities (10,1) etc? The prompt doesn't explicitly say they captured them yet, just that they are "six armies ashore". But victory requires enemy holds zero *cities*.
                If TF-5 is on land adjacent to city (11,1), they need to ENTER it to capture.
                Combat Engine resolves combat. I am commander.

                Also "Conquest is won by taking cities... press outward while you can."
                And "A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have made wrong".

                TF-5 needs to consolidate and push, OR secure cities.

                Given Standing Doctrine: "One force cannot hold your cities and conquer the enemy's at once."
                But here I am attacking enemy cities.

                They just launched.
                Doctrine also: "Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first".
                Where are my reserves? UNASSIGNED `i` #11(1,0), `j`#12(3,0), `k`#13(1,1), `l`#15(4,3) in city. `m`#17(3,4). `n`#18(2,0).
                `i` and `j` are near TF-2 (at 2,0).
                TF-2 is Capital. Needs defense.
                Doctrine: "As your strength grows, divide it: a garrison, a spearhead, an eye."
                I need to ensure the capital (2,0) holds while TF-5 fights.
                Also TF-5 has Transports `o` and `p` offshore. They can go into sea at (9,1)/(9,2).
                But they are empty. Why leave them there? Maybe waiting for supply or to move back if needed.
                Wait, Doctrine: "Transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with a warship... or it waits."
                Do I know of enemy warships? Map only shows `~` (water) and fog. Units are Land.
                Is (9,3) safe? It's adjacent to `q`. Wait, Destroyer is mine. Is the sea contested? "an enemy army garrisons (11,1)".
                If I assume no warships are active nearby, `q` might be needed for escorting future landings or just protection.
                But TF-5's armies are *already on land* ashore at t56. Transports left empty.
                Objective: CAPTURE (11,1).
                Doctrine: "Capture attempts consume armies... Assault with spares."
                I need to send enough units to ensure success without wasting everything if the enemy holds out or reinforcements arrive.
                But I can't commit ALL of TF-5's 6 armies and then have none left for next phase if (11,1) is just one city.
                Doctrine: "Conquest is won by taking cities... press outward while you can."
                If I order RETASK CAPTURE (11,1), it consumes the armies to fight.
                Better idea: Keep TF-5 focused on capture. Ensure they have enough spares. They have 6 units for 1 city target? Maybe overkill but safe.
                However, TF-5's current objective is "strike east". The enemy garrison (11,1) is the focus now.
                If I don't move them, they are stuck landing.

                If I order TF-5 RETASK CAPTURE (11,1), they engage.
                But Doctrine: "A general blind past his own walls is losing already." Keep an eye on capital.
                Capital (2,0) is defended by TF-2 (`a`, `b`).
                Are there threats? No contact reported yet for TF-2.
                TF-5 is the spearhead. It's at Turn 56. The objective t54 was "take the enemy cities". Plural. There is another city (11,2) listed under KNOWN ENEMY. And map shows `E` at some point.
                So capturing (11,1) might open the way to (11,2).
                But TF-5 needs supplies for next phase? They have Transports empty offshore. Maybe send them back or keep them ready.
                Wait, Doctrine: "Cities are your only source of force." Need to rebuild.
                Do I need to Reinforce TF-2? Capital is secure ("garrisoned; no contact"). Uncommitted reserves (`i`, `j`) are nearby (1,0), (3,0). They could reinforce TF-2 if needed, or move with TF-5 later.

                1. TF-2: Keep DEFEND (2,0). It works. No need to change unless contact.
                2. TF-5: They have 6 armies ready at land border of target (11,1). Need to engage capture. Doctrine says "Assault with spares". I have more than one city's worth of armies potentially nearby or just here. But if the city is (11,1), and I order CAPTURE, the engines resolve it. If they lose all 6, TF-5 fails.
                   Better to keep them *tasked* with CAPTURE but ensure reinforcements are ready? Or do I need to move Transports inland? No, armies are already there.
                   Wait, "transports empty offshore". Why not move them back to supply if needed? But Doctrine says "Never stage land units on water." Armies are ashore. Transports can go anywhere safe.
                   If I order TF-5 to RETASK CAPTURE (11,1), it commits the armies to combat resolution logic for that turn/turns. Since capture consumes armies, I need to make sure they survive or return. But if they succeed, great.
                   Wait, "One force cannot hold your cities and conquer the enemy's at once."
                   If TF-5 takes (11,1), it must move *forward* to attack next city or move troops back for production.
                   Doctrine: "Conquest is won by taking cities... press outward".
                   I should keep the offensive intent but ensure logistics.
                   However, looking at TF-2, they are holding. If TF-5 wins, TF-2 gets pressure.
                   Is there a need to reinforce TF-2? `i` and `j` are right there (1,0) and (3,0). Can I send them to TF-2 to strengthen garrison?
                   Doctrine: "As your strength grows, divide it...".
                   If the enemy army is at (11,1), they might need support from other cities.
                   But `i` and `j` are currently UNASSIGNED near my capital.
                   I can order TF-2 REINFORCE to strengthen? No, `TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS`.
                   Wait, if I issue orders now (Turn 56):
                   - TF-2: CONTINUE DEFEND (2,0).
                   - TF-5: RETASK CAPTURE (11,1). (They are already positioned).
                   Is there any need to move transports `o` and `p`? They are empty. Doctrine says "A transport caught by a warship is destroyed." If sea is safe, they can wait. If I have no threat of water combat (no enemy ships shown), leave them to support future operations or return later.
                   Wait, I need to check if `TF-5` already has the objective of "CAPTURE (11,1)". Yes, Current Taskings: "TF-5 formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1)". So it's still current. Why amend?
                   Because Doctrine says: "A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have made wrong".
                   The event is that they are *ashore* and *ready*. The objective matches.
                   Maybe I need to RETASK them to push further once (11,1) is captured? But I can't know yet.
                   Wait, look at UNASSIGNED units `l` #15 in city (4,3). This is my 4th city. "building ARMY, 1 turn left".
                   If TF-5 takes the capital east, I need a secure rear?
                   Maybe I should reinforce TF-2 to make sure capital is impregnable. `i` and `j` are there. Move them to TF-2 as REINFORCEMENT.
                   "Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first". If enemy comes at (11,1) from the west? They are east of me.
                   But if they push back towards (2,0), I need strength there.
                   However, TF-5 is already far away on the coast (9-12 range).
                   Doctrine: "One force cannot hold your cities and conquer...".
                   TF-2 holds city 1. TF-5 attacks city 3/4.
                   If I reinforce TF-2, it makes it safer. `i` (1,0) and `j` (3,0). Move them to TF-2?
                   Wait, units list says `i #11` at (1,0). TF-2 is Defending (2,0). Distance is small (1 step).
                   If I send `i` and `j` to join TF-2, does it strengthen the defense?
                   Yes. "Garrisoned; no contact". Secure it.
                   Wait, `TF-5` has Transports offshore. Should they be moved back to city (4,1) or something? No cities nearby except maybe if I move them. But land units can't stage there.
                   Actually, looking at the map:
                   `E` is at row 1 col 6? "r1 ... c E". Wait, `c` #10 army in TF-5 is not at E. `E` is enemy city.
                   If there is an Enemy City (E) at r1c6 (col 6, row 1). Unit `h`#8 is at (12,3)? No (12,3) is col 3? Wait, coordinates.
                   Let's re-read map carefully.
                   r0: . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ? (Col 0 to 7?)
                   Counting spaces: `.` ` ` `i` ` ` `O` ` ` `j`. That's 5 chars before `~`.
